name: Santoña Marshes Natural Park Loop
name_original: Parque Natural de las Marismas de Santoña, Victoria y Joyel – ruta circular
description: Explore the easy 14.2-mile mountain bike route through Parque Natural de las Marismas de Santoña, Victoria y Joyel, perfect for birdwatching
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Santoña offer a diverse landscape for exploration, characterized by its coastal setting, the extensive Santoña, Victoria y Joyel Marshes Natural Park, and the prominent Mount Buciero. The region features a mix of coastal paths, marshland routes, and forest trails, providing varied terrain for mountain bikers. These routes often wind through protected natural areas and offer views of the Cantabrian Sea and the surrounding bay. The elevation changes are generally moderate, with some challenging climbs on Mount Buciero. Best mountain bike trails around Santoña The most popular mountain bike trail is Santoña Marshes Natural Park Loop, a 14.2 miles (22.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the ecologically rich wetlands and coastal holm oak forests. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Marisma de Solija – View of the Coastal Cliffs loop from Santoña, a difficult 29.4 miles (47.3 km) path. This route offers expansive views of the rugged coastal cliffs and the Cantabrian Sea. Local mountain bikers also love the Eucalyptus Trail – View of Noja and the coast loop from Argoños, a 21.8 miles (35.1 km) trail leading through eucalyptus forests, often completed in about 2 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking around Santoña is defined by coastal paths, marshland trails, and forest routes on Mount Buciero. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops through natural parks to more challenging climbs with panoramic views. The routes in Santoña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 1100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Santoña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Santoña?
A: The Santoña region offers a diverse mix of terrain for mountain biking. You'll find coastal paths with sea views, trails winding through the extensive Santoña, Victoria y Joyel Marshes Natural Park, and forest routes on Mount Buciero. This variety provides options from relatively flat marshland rides to more challenging climbs with panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Santoña?
A: Yes, Santoña has several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000948576/santona-marshes-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santoña Marshes Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 14.2-mile (22.8 km) route that explores the ecologically rich wetlands and coastal holm oak forests with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Santoña?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10509321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marisma de Solija – View of the Coastal Cliffs loop from Santoña&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 29.4-mile (47.3 km) path offers significant elevation changes and expansive views of the rugged coastal cliffs and the Cantabrian Sea. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40335088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro del Pescador – View of Playa de Berria loop from Penal de El Dueso&lt;/a&gt;, which includes challenging climbs on Mount Buciero.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Santoña?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Santoña are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000948576/santona-marshes-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santoña Marshes Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38186466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eucalyptus Trail – View of Noja and the coast loop from Argoños&lt;/a&gt; are excellent examples of circular routes.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see while mountain biking in Santoña?
A: Mountain biking in Santoña offers access to stunning natural landmarks and viewpoints. You can encounter features like the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1834895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4545693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ojerada Caves&lt;/a&gt;, or reach summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4486690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solpico Summit&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views. Many routes also offer views of the Cantabrian Sea and the Santoña bay, especially from the slopes of Mount Buciero.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in Santoña?
A: For families, the easier routes through the Santoña, Victoria y Joyel Marshes Natural Park are ideal. These trails are generally flatter and offer a pleasant experience for all ages, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty without overly strenuous climbs.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Santoña?
A: The best season for mountain biking in Santoña is generally spring and autumn when the weather is mild and pleasant. Summers can be warm, especially on exposed coastal paths, while winters can bring more rain, making some trails muddy. However, the diverse terrain means there are usually suitable options year-round, with forest trails offering shade in summer and marshland routes being less affected by light rain.

Q: Are mountain bike trails in Santoña dog-friendly?
A: Many natural trails in the Santoña region, particularly those in the Santoña, Victoria y Joyel Marshes Natural Park and on Mount Buciero, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected natural areas to protect wildlife, and to check specific trail regulations before you go.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Santoña?
A: The mountain bike trails around Santoña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 130 reviews. Riders often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from coastal views to marshland and forest trails, and appreciate the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting while mountain biking in Santoña?
A: Yes, the Santoña, Victoria y Joyel Marshes Natural Park is one of the most ecologically valuable wetlands in northern Spain and a crucial stop for migratory birds. Mountain biking through this area, such as on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000948576/santona-marshes-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santoña Marshes Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offers excellent opportunities for birdwatching and observing other marshland wildlife.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails near Santoña typically take to complete?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Santoña varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000948576/santona-marshes-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santoña Marshes Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; (14.2 miles / 22.8 km) can take around 1 hour 23 minutes, while more difficult and longer trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10509321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marisma de Solija – View of the Coastal Cliffs loop from Santoña&lt;/a&gt; (29.4 miles / 47.3 km), might take over 3 hours.
tips: This natural park is the most important wetland on the Cantabrian coast. Its 6,679 hectares are full of unique marine fauna and are home to more than 121 species of waterfowl. The tides constantly change this landscape that can be seen from the different shores and from the bridge that crosses it.

Belonging to the Order of the Capuchin Fathers, this beautiful monastery was founded by the Franciscans at the beginning of the 14th century. Later it was enlarged until it was sold after the French invasions of the 19th century and finally recovered later by the Order.

Square tower, about 9 m. sideways. It has masonry walls except in the openings that are made of ashlar masonry. It is topped by square battlements. The main facade is the southern one, with an ogival arch entrance. It has a large window with a semicircular arch, which gave access to a wooden balcony supported by four shelves. On the second floor there are two windows with ogival arches and two rectangular embrasures with spillage towards the interior. In the other facades it also has pointed openings.
https://turismodecantabria.com/disfrutala/what-to-visit/194-torre-de-venero

The Solija marsh is located within the Natural Park and is a protected area with a flora and fauna reserve. There are several paths to go through them and some bird watching booths for those who enjoy this activity.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route is rated as easy, making it suitable for beginners and those looking for a relaxed ride. It covers approximately 22.8 km with a modest elevation gain of about 172 meters, typically taking around 1 hour and 23 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally easy, with sections that are mostly paved, making it accessible for various skill levels. You'll encounter paths through the extensive wetlands, offering a unique natural landscape. While some routes in the broader park area might have unpaved sections, this specific circular route is designed to be manageable.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a chance to explore the rich natural environment of the park. You'll pass by the significant Santoña Marshes and the Marisma de Solija, which are crucial wetland ecosystems. Additionally, you can spot historical landmarks like the Monastery of San Sebastián de Montehano and the Venero Tower.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The Parque Natural de las Marismas de Santoña, Victoria y Joyel is a vital habitat, especially for birdwatching. You can expect to see a wide variety of waterfowl, as the park is home to over 130 species. Keep an eye out for diverse wetland ecosystems and the unique flora of the marshes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The park is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and are ideal for birdwatching, as migratory species are abundant. Summer can be warm, so an early start is recommended. Winter rides are also possible, but be prepared for cooler, potentially wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for this exact starting point are not provided, but the natural park has various access points and parking areas. It's advisable to check local maps or park information centers for the most convenient parking near the start of the circular route, especially in Santoña or nearby towns.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Regulations regarding dogs in natural parks can vary. While many natural areas in Spain allow dogs, they are typically required to be on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always check specific signage at the trailhead or the park's official website for the most current rules regarding pets.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Parque Natural de las Marismas de Santoña, Victoria y Joyel?
faqs.answer: Generally, there is no entrance fee to access the Parque Natural de las Marismas de Santoña, Victoria y Joyel. However, certain activities or specific areas within the park might require permits or have particular regulations. For mountain biking, it's usually free, but always respect park rules and signage.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will ride along parts of the Senda del Litoral, the Northern Route (Cantabria), the Sendero de la Marisma de Solija, the Camino al castillo de Montehano, and the Diques de Bengoa.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is located near Santoña and other towns, so you'll find various amenities. There are cafes, restaurants, and other services available in the surrounding areas where you can refuel before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Can I rent a mountain bike in Santoña?
faqs.answer: Yes, Santoña and other nearby towns often have shops that offer bicycle rentals, including mountain bikes. It's a good idea to search online or inquire locally upon arrival to find the best rental options for your needs.
